segments.text: First listen and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Do you think that the writer passed his driving test?

translations.zh-Hans: 你认为作者通过驾驶考试了吗？

segments.text: I was being tested for a driving license for the third time.

translations.zh-Hans: 我正在第三次参加驾驶执照考试。

segments.text: I had been asked to drive in heavy traffic and had done so successfully.

translations.zh-Hans: 考官要求我在拥挤的车流中驾驶，我顺利地做到了。

segments.text: After having been instructed to drive out of town, I began to acquire confidence.

translations.zh-Hans: 接到把车开出城的指令后，我开始有了信心。

segments.text: Sure that I had passed, I was almost beginning to enjoy my test.

translations.zh-Hans: 我确信自己已经通过考试，甚至开始享受这次考试了。

segments.text: The examiner must have been pleased with my performance, for he smiled and said, Just one more thing, Mr.

translations.zh-Hans: 考官一定对我的表现很满意，因为他微笑着说：“还有最后一项，艾姆斯先生。”

segments.text: Let us suppose that a child suddenly crosses the road in front of you.

translations.zh-Hans: “假设一个孩子突然从你前面横穿马路。”

segments.text: As soon as I tap on the window, you must stop within five feet.

translations.zh-Hans: “我一敲车窗，你就必须在五英尺之内把车停下。”

segments.text: I continued driving and after some time the examiner tapped loudly.

translations.zh-Hans: 我继续驾车，过了一会儿，考官重重地敲了敲车窗。

segments.text: Though the sound could be heard clearly, it took me a long time to react.

translations.zh-Hans: 虽然敲击声听得很清楚，我却过了很久才反应过来。

segments.text: I suddenly pressed the brake pedal hard and we were both thrown forward.

translations.zh-Hans: 我猛踩刹车踏板，我们俩都被甩向前方。

segments.text: The examiner looked at me sadly.
he said, in a mournful voice, you have just killed that child.

translations.zh-Hans: 他用悲哀的语调说：“你刚刚撞死了那个孩子。”
